notes: Time word; placed before the verb.

Used with verbs of motion. "chhut-lâi"=come out; "chhut-khì"=go out. At the end of a clause/sentence, "chhut" does not change tone ("lâi"/"khì" are enclitics); mid-clause it follows the regular sandhi rules.
Frequently used with modifiers denoting undesirable qualities. Negative: "bē" 袂.
Frequently means "think"/"in my opinion" rather than "look with the eyes".

english: be good to + Verb; be easy to + Verb

notes: May be used for "match", "a light" (for tobacco), or as a short word for "electric light".

Used with verbs of motion. "ji̍p-lâi"=come in; "ji̍p-khì"=go in. At the end of a clause/sentence, "ji̍p" does not change tone ("lâi"/"khì" are enclitics); mid-clause it follows the regular sandhi rules.
Name of city/county in southern Taiwan.
